summary: Short story by Annie Proulx (published 1997, collected in Close Range: Wyoming Stories, 1999) and 2005 film directed by Ang Lee. The story follows Jack Twist (rodeo cowboy) and Ennis del Mar (rancher) who fall in love while herding sheep for Joe Aguirre near the title Wyoming mountain as teenagers. 'I wish I knew how to quit you.' Larry McMurtry and Diana Ossana wrote the screenplay (McMurtry won Academy Award). The film won the Venice Golden Lion but lost Best Picture to Crash. Stars Heath Ledger and Jake Gyllenhaal.

works.name: The Short Story (Annie Proulx, 1997)
works.description: Annie Proulx's short story was first published in 1997 and collected in Close Range: Wyoming Stories (1999). Jack Twist (rodeo cowboy) and Ennis del Mar (rancher) meet as teenagers herding sheep for Joe Aguirre at the title Wyoming mountain. 'I wish I knew how to quit you' is the famous line. Alma Beers (Ennis's wife) divorces him after noticing he doesn't use his fishing pole on trips (inferring his time away with Jack). The story ends with Ennis finding Jack's blood-stained shirt in a closet and pinning a postcard to it — Ennis believes Jack was killed with a tire iron. The final line notes an 'open space' between what a man 'knew' and 'tried to believe' that 'nothing could be done about.' The story was published a year before the murder of Matthew Shepard in Wyoming.

clues.clue: Jack Twist and Ennis del Mar meet as teenagers herding sheep for Joe Aguirre; 'I wish I knew how to quit you'; Alma Beers divorces Ennis after noticing he doesn't use his fishing pole
examples: After discussing leaving for Mexico, a man in this story says 'I wish I knew how to quit you.'
In this story, Alma Beers divorces her husband after noticing that he doesn't use his pole on fishing trips.
As teenagers, this story's protagonists work for Joe Aguirre at the title location, where they take turns cooking meals and herding sheep.

clues.clue: Final scene: Ennis pins a postcard to Jack's blood-stained shirt; final line: 'open space' between what a man 'knew' and 'tried to believe' that 'nothing could be done about'
examples: A man in this story pins a postcard to a blood-stained shirt in honor of a character he thinks was killed with a tire iron.
This story's final line notes an 'open space' between what a man 'knew' and 'tried to believe' that 'nothing could be done about.'

clues.clue: Collected in Close Range: Wyoming Stories (1999); published a year before Matthew Shepard's murder in Wyoming; Larry McMurtry and Diana Ossana adapted it for film (McMurtry won Oscar)
examples: Larry McMurty won an Academy Award for co-writing a film adaptation of this story, which is collected in 1999's Close Range: Wyoming Stories.
This Annie Proulx story, also set in Wyoming, was published a year before the murder of Matthew Shepard.

works.name: The Film (Ang Lee, 2005)
works.description: Directed by Ang Lee; screenplay by Larry McMurtry and Diana Ossana. Stars Heath Ledger (Ennis del Mar) and Jake Gyllenhaal (Jack Twist). Cinematography by Rodrigo Prieto. Won the Golden Lion at Venice 2005. Lost the Academy Award for Best Picture to Crash ('inexplicably'). Heath Ledger won the MTV Movie Award for Best Kiss with Gyllenhaal; Ledger later won a posthumous Oscar for playing the Joker in The Dark Knight. In the film's final scene, Ennis clutches Jack's old jacket in a closet and cries. B. Ruby Rich identified the film as changing the direction of New Queer Cinema. Charles Wuorinen composed an opera based on Proulx's story. Ang Lee also directed Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon and Sense and Sensibility.

clues.clue: Directed by Ang Lee; screenplay by Larry McMurtry (Oscar) and Diana Ossana; Heath Ledger and Jake Gyllenhaal; cinematographer Rodrigo Prieto
examples: McMurtry and Diana Ossana wrote the film adaptation of this story by Annie Proulx, in which the cowboy Ennis del Mar dreams about his romance with Jack Twist.
A cinematographer with this first name shot films like Silence and Brokeback Mountain as part of his collaborations with Ang Lee and Martin Scorsese.

clues.clue: Won Golden Lion at Venice 2005; lost Best Picture Oscar to Crash; Heath Ledger won MTV Movie Award for Best Kiss with Gyllenhaal; Ledger later won posthumous Oscar for Joker
examples: In 2005, this city's film festival awarded the Golden Lion to Brokeback Mountain.
Inexplicably, Brokeback Mountain lost the 2005 Best Picture Oscar to this film about racial tensions in Los Angeles.
This actor won the 2006 award for his kiss with Jake Gyllenhaal in the cowboy romance Brokeback Mountain. He won a posthumous Oscar for playing the Joker in The Dark Knight.

clues.clue: Final scene: Ennis clutches Jack's old jacket and cries; B. Ruby Rich identified it as changing the direction of New Queer Cinema
examples: Ennis clutches Jack's old jacket in a closet and performs this action in the final scene of Brokeback Mountain.
Rich later identified this 2005 Ang Lee film as changing the direction of New Queer Cinema.

comprehensive_summary:
Brokeback Mountain is both an Annie Proulx short story (1997, collected in Close Range: Wyoming Stories, 1999) and a 2005 film directed by Ang Lee. The story follows Jack Twist (rodeo cowboy) and Ennis del Mar (rancher) who fall in love as teenagers while herding sheep for Joe Aguirre near the title Wyoming mountain. The famous line 'I wish I knew how to quit you' occurs when the men discuss leaving for Mexico. Alma Beers, Ennis's wife, divorces him after noticing he doesn't use his fishing pole on trips (inferring his time with Jack). The story ends with Ennis finding Jack's blood-stained shirt in a closet and pinning a postcard to it — Ennis believes Jack was killed with a tire iron. The final line notes an 'open space' between what a man 'knew' and 'tried to believe' that 'nothing could be done about.' The story was published a year before Matthew Shepard's murder in Wyoming.

Larry McMurtry and Diana Ossana wrote the screenplay; McMurtry won an Academy Award for it. The film stars Heath Ledger (Ennis) and Jake Gyllenhaal (Jack); cinematography by Rodrigo Prieto. It won the Golden Lion at Venice 2005 but lost Best Picture at the Oscars to Crash ('inexplicably'). Heath Ledger won the MTV Movie Award for Best Kiss with Gyllenhaal; Ledger later won a posthumous Oscar for playing the Joker in The Dark Knight. The film's final scene shows Ennis clutching Jack's old jacket in a closet and crying. B. Ruby Rich identified the film as changing the direction of New Queer Cinema. Charles Wuorinen composed an opera based on Proulx's story.
recursive_suggestions: Annie Proulx — author of the source story; also wrote The Shipping News (Literature)
Ang Lee — director; also directed Crouching Tiger Hidden Dragon, Sense and Sensibility, Lust Caution (Fine Arts)
Heath Ledger — star of the film; won posthumous Oscar for Joker in The Dark Knight (Fine Arts)
